steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank closes a direct quotation from Alfred Nobel and must connect it to the next independent clause ("in 2011, for instance, judges recognized..."). The closing punctuation must end the quote correctly and join the two independent clauses properly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "direction";": Closes the quotation with a closing quotation mark, then uses a semicolon to join the two independent clauses. Both parts are correct: the quote is properly closed and the semicolon is the right separator. ✓
  • "direction" and,": Uses "and" with a comma, which would make the second clause a dependent continuation rather than a full independent clause properly linked. "And" followed by a comma before a subject creates a comma splice variant; a semicolon is required here. ✗
  • "direction": Closes the quote but provides no punctuation to separate two independent clauses, creating a run-on sentence. ✗
  • "direction,": Does not close the quotation mark at all, leaving the quote open and attaching the next clause as if it were still part of Alfred Nobel's words. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
